title: Debug a leaking or duplicate subscription
description: Trace duplicated side effects or growing memory to a subscription that is never torn down.
body: Steps: 1. Reproduce by navigating into and out of the component repeatedly and watch a side effect or memory grow. 2. Locate every .subscribe() in the component/service. 3. For each, check whether it is the async pipe (managed), takeUntilDestroyed/destroy-Subject (managed), or bare (leaking). 4. Convert bare long-lived subscribes to the async pipe or add takeUntilDestroyed. 5. Re-navigate and confirm emissions stop after destroy. Rollback point: before changing the subscription model. Validation: a test asserts no callback fires after ngOnDestroy and memory stabilizes across navigations.
